metadata.description: Environment name — used in resource naming (e.g. dev, staging, prod, uat, sandbox)
Azure region for resource deployment
Resource group for FinOps governance resources
FinOps team email for alert routing
Teams incoming webhook URL for budget alert channel
Default budget amount for new resource groups (EUR)
Monthly subscription-level budget (EUR)
Tags to apply to all resources
Enable amortized cost pipeline (Function App, Log Analytics, Alert Rules, Workbook)
Enable auto-budget Logic App for new RG creation
Enable self-service budget change Logic App
Enable policy definition and assignment (requires Resource Policy Contributor)
Enable RBAC role assignments for Logic App managed identities (requires User Access Administrator)
Function App host key for Cosmos sync from budget-change Logic App (set post-deploy)
Budget start date (first of current month). Once a budget is created, the start date cannot be changed — delete and recreate instead.
Cost tracking scope: resourceGroup (per-RG only), subscription (sub-level only), or both (RG + subscription rollup)
Custom name for the Action Group (leave blank for default: ag-finops-budget-alerts)
Custom name for the Cosmos DB account (leave blank for auto-generated unique name)
Custom name for the Storage Account (leave blank for auto-generated unique name)
Custom name for the Function App (leave blank for auto-generated unique name)
Custom name for the Auto-Budget Logic App (leave blank for default: la-finops-auto-budget)
Custom name for the Budget Change Logic App (leave blank for default: la-finops-budget-change)
Custom name for the Backfill Logic App (leave blank for default: la-finops-backfill)
Custom name for the Log Analytics workspace (leave blank for default: law-finops-budget)
Custom name for the Workbook (leave blank for default: FinOps Budget Compliance Dashboard)
Custom name for the HeadUp alert rule (leave blank for default: finops-alert-headup)
Custom name for the Warning alert rule (leave blank for default: finops-alert-warning)
Custom name for the Critical alert rule (leave blank for default: finops-alert-critical)
Custom name for the post-deploy managed identity (leave blank for default: id-finops-post-deploy)
Enable private networking — deploys VNet, private endpoints for Cosmos DB & Storage, and Function App VNet integration
Enable post-deploy automation script (code deploy + kickstart). Disable if subscription has restrictive storage key policies — use CI/CD instead.
VNet name (only used when enablePrivateNetworking = true)
VNet address space (only used when enablePrivateNetworking = true)
FinOps team email(s) — comma-separated for multiple recipients (e.g. "team@org.com,lead@org.com")
Budget name
Monthly budget amount in billing currency
Scope: subscription or resourceGroup
Resource group name (required if scope is resourceGroup)
FinOps team email
RG owner email (for per-threshold routing)
BU lead email (added at 75%+)
Action Group resource ID
Budget start date (YYYY-MM-01 format). Once created, this cannot change — delete and recreate the budget to use a different start date.
Budget end date

properties.displayName: FinOps: Audit Resource Groups Without Budget
properties.description: Flags resource groups that do not have a Microsoft.Consumption/budgets resource. FinOps baseline requirement for cost governance.

metadata.description: AuditIfNotExists flags non-compliance without blocking.
Retention in days (30 = free tier)
Log Analytics workspace resource ID
Action Group resource ID for alert routing

properties.displayName: FinOps HeadUp — RGs at 60%+ budget utilization
properties.description: Resource groups where amortized spend has reached 60% of budget. Early awareness for FinOps team.

allOf.query:
FinOpsInventory_CL
            | where actualPct_d >= 60 and actualPct_d < 80
            | where complianceStatus_s != "critical"
            | project resourceGroup_s, actualPct_d, forecastPct_d, amortizedMTD_d, technicalBudget_d, complianceStatus_s, TimeGenerated
            | order by actualPct_d desc

properties.displayName: FinOps Warning — RGs at 80%+ budget utilization
properties.description: Resource groups where amortized spend has reached 80% of budget. Action needed to prevent overspend.

allOf.query:
FinOpsInventory_CL
            | where actualPct_d >= 80 and actualPct_d < 95
            | project resourceGroup_s, actualPct_d, forecastPct_d, amortizedMTD_d, technicalBudget_d, complianceStatus_s, TimeGenerated
            | order by actualPct_d desc

properties.displayName: FinOps Critical — RGs at 95%+ budget utilization
properties.description: Resource groups where amortized spend has reached 95% of budget. Immediate action required.

allOf.query:
FinOpsInventory_CL
            | where actualPct_d >= 95
            | project resourceGroup_s, actualPct_d, forecastPct_d, amortizedMTD_d, technicalBudget_d, complianceStatus_s, TimeGenerated
            | order by actualPct_d desc

metadata.description: Resource ID of the Log Analytics workspace containing FinOpsInventory_CL

variables.kqlSubscriptionSummary: FinOpsInventory_CL | where scope_s == 'subscription' or resourceGroup_s == '_subscription_rollup' | summarize arg_max(TimeGenerated, *) | project SubscriptionBudget=technicalBudget_d, AmortizedMTD=amortizedMTD_d, ForecastEOM=forecastEOM_d, BudgetUsed=actualPct_d, ForecastPct=forecastPct_d, Status=complianceStatus_s, TotalRGs=totalResourceGroups_d, LastEvaluated=lastEvaluated_s
variables.kqlCompliancePie: FinOpsInventory_CL | where resourceGroup_s != '_subscription_rollup' and isnotempty(resourceGroup_s) | summarize arg_max(TimeGenerated, *) by resourceGroup_s | summarize Count=count() by complianceStatus_s | order by Count desc
variables.kqlInventoryGrid: FinOpsInventory_CL | where resourceGroup_s != '_subscription_rollup' and isnotempty(resourceGroup_s) | summarize arg_max(TimeGenerated, *) by resourceGroup_s | project ResourceGroup=resourceGroup_s, Budget=technicalBudget_d, SpendMTD=amortizedMTD_d, Forecast=forecastEOM_d, UsedPct=actualPct_d, ForecastPct=forecastPct_d, Status=complianceStatus_s, Tier=spendTier_s, Owner=ownerEmail_s, CostCenter=costCenter_s, LastEvaluated=lastEvaluated_s | order by UsedPct desc
variables.kqlTopSpenders: FinOpsInventory_CL | where resourceGroup_s != '_subscription_rollup' and isnotempty(resourceGroup_s) | summarize arg_max(TimeGenerated, *) by resourceGroup_s | top 10 by amortizedMTD_d desc | project ResourceGroup=resourceGroup_s, SpendMTD=amortizedMTD_d, Budget=technicalBudget_d, UsedPct=actualPct_d, Status=complianceStatus_s
variables.kqlVarianceReport: FinOpsInventory_CL | where resourceGroup_s != '_subscription_rollup' and isnotempty(resourceGroup_s) | summarize arg_max(TimeGenerated, *) by resourceGroup_s | where financeBudget_d > 0 or technicalBudget_d > 0 | extend FinanceVariance = amortizedMTD_d - financeBudget_d, TechVariance = amortizedMTD_d - technicalBudget_d | project ResourceGroup=resourceGroup_s, FinanceBudget=financeBudget_d, TechBudget=technicalBudget_d, SpendMTD=amortizedMTD_d, FinanceVariance, TechVariance, Status=complianceStatus_s | order by FinanceVariance desc
variables.kqlBurnRate: FinOpsInventory_CL | where resourceGroup_s != '_subscription_rollup' and isnotempty(resourceGroup_s) | summarize arg_max(TimeGenerated, *) by resourceGroup_s | where burnRateDaily_d > 0 | project ResourceGroup=resourceGroup_s, DailyBurn=burnRateDaily_d, MonthlyProjection=burnRateDaily_d*30, Budget=technicalBudget_d, DaysToExhaust=iff(burnRateDaily_d>0, (technicalBudget_d-amortizedMTD_d)/burnRateDaily_d, 999.0) | order by DaysToExhaust asc

properties.serializedData: [concat('{"version":"Notebook/1.0","items":[{"type":1,"content":{"json":"# FinOps Budget Compliance Dashboard\n\nReal-time amortized cost tracking powered by Log Analytics (FinOpsInventory_CL). Data refreshes after each daily evaluation (06:00 UTC) and on-demand triggers."},"name":"title"},{"type":3,"content":{"version":"KqlItem/1.0","query":"', variables('kqlSubscriptionSummary'), '","size":4,"title":"Subscription Summary","queryType":0,"resourceType":"microsoft.operationalinsights/workspaces","crossComponentResources":["', parameters('workspaceResourceId'), '"],"visualization":"tiles","tileSettings":{"showBorder":true}},"name":"subscription-summary"},{"type":3,"content":{"version":"KqlItem/1.0","query":"', variables('kqlCompliancePie'), '","size":2,"title":"Compliance Status Distribution","queryType":0,"resourceType":"microsoft.operationalinsights/workspaces","crossComponentResources":["', parameters('workspaceResourceId'), '"],"visualization":"piechart"},"name":"compliance-pie"},{"type":3,"content":{"version":"KqlItem/1.0","query":"', variables('kqlTopSpenders'), '","size":0,"title":"Top 10 Spenders (by Amortized MTD)","queryType":0,"resourceType":"microsoft.operationalinsights/workspaces","crossComponentResources":["', parameters('workspaceResourceId'), '"],"visualization":"barchart"},"name":"top-spenders"},{"type":3,"content":{"version":"KqlItem/1.0","query":"', variables('kqlInventoryGrid'), '","size":0,"title":"Full Inventory - All Resource Groups","queryType":0,"resourceType":"microsoft.operationalinsights/workspaces","crossComponentResources":["', parameters('workspaceResourceId'), '"],"visualization":"table","gridSettings":{"sortBy":[{"itemKey":"UsedPct","sortOrder":2}]}},"name":"inventory-grid"},{"type":3,"content":{"version":"KqlItem/1.0","query":"', variables('kqlVarianceReport'), '","size":0,"title":"Finance vs Technical Budget Variance","queryType":0,"resourceType":"microsoft.operationalinsights/workspaces","crossComponentResources":["', parameters('workspaceResourceId'), '"],"visualization":"table"},"name":"variance-report"},{"type":3,"content":{"version":"KqlItem/1.0","query":"', variables('kqlBurnRate'), '","size":0,"title":"Burn Rate Analysis - Days to Budget Exhaustion","queryType":0,"resourceType":"microsoft.operationalinsights/workspaces","crossComponentResources":["', parameters('workspaceResourceId'), '"],"visualization":"table","gridSettings":{"sortBy":[{"itemKey":"DaysToExhaust","sortOrder":1}]}},"name":"burn-rate"}],"isLocked":false}')]

metadata.description: Name of the Data Collection Endpoint
Name of the Data Collection Rule
Package URI: blob URL to the zip package in storage (post-deploy script uploads it)
Set to false if deployer lacks User Access Administrator role
Subscription budget amount (for subscription-level rollup)
Cost tracking scope: resourceGroup, subscription, or both

properties.scriptContent:
#!/bin/bash
      set -e

      echo "=== FinOps Post-Deploy Kickstart ==="
      echo "Subscription: $SUBSCRIPTION_ID"
      echo "Function App: $FUNCTION_APP_NAME"
      echo "Storage: $STORAGE_ACCOUNT_NAME"
      echo ""

      # ── Step 0: Ensure resource providers are registered ──
      echo "[0/7] Registering resource providers..."
      for provider in Microsoft.CostManagement Microsoft.Consumption Microsoft.DocumentDB Microsoft.EventGrid Microsoft.Logic Microsoft.Insights Microsoft.Web Microsoft.OperationalInsights Microsoft.ManagedIdentity; do
        az provider register --namespace "$provider" --wait false --output none 2>/dev/null || true
      done
      echo "  Resource providers registration initiated"
      echo ""

      # ── Step 1: Create amortized cost export ──────────────
      echo "[1/7] Creating daily amortized cost export..."
      START_DATE=$(date -u +"%Y-%m-01T00:00:00")
      END_DATE=$(date -u -d "+1 year" +"%Y-12-31T00:00:00" 2>/dev/null || date -u -v+1y +"%Y-12-31T00:00:00")

      EXPORT_BODY=$(cat <<EOF
      {
        "properties": {
          "schedule": {
            "status": "Active",
            "recurrence": "Daily",
            "recurrencePeriod": {
              "from": "$START_DATE",
              "to": "$END_DATE"
            }
          },
          "format": "Csv",
          "deliveryInfo": {
            "destination": {
              "resourceId": "$STORAGE_ACCOUNT_ID",
              "container": "amortized-cost-exports",
              "rootFolderPath": "exports"
            }
          },
          "definition": {
            "type": "AmortizedCost",
            "timeframe": "MonthToDate"
          }
        }
      }
EOF
      )

      az rest --method PUT \
        --url "https://management.azure.com/subscriptions/$SUBSCRIPTION_ID/providers/Microsoft.CostManagement/exports/finops-daily-amortized?api-version=2023-11-01" \
        --body "$EXPORT_BODY" \
        --output none 2>/dev/null || echo "  Export may already exist or requires Cost Management Contributor"

      echo "  Export created: finops-daily-amortized"

      # Trigger immediate export run
      az rest --method POST \
        --url "https://management.azure.com/subscriptions/$SUBSCRIPTION_ID/providers/Microsoft.CostManagement/exports/finops-daily-amortized/run?api-version=2023-11-01" \
        --output none 2>/dev/null || echo "  Could not trigger immediate run"

      echo "  Triggered immediate export (data arrives in ~5 min)"
      echo ""

      # ── Step 2: Deploy Function App code via blob upload ──
      echo "[2/7] Deploying Function App code to blob storage..."
      PACKAGE_URL="https://raw.githubusercontent.com/gitpavleenbali/azure-amortized-cost-management/main/functions/amortized-budget-engine.zip"

      # Download zip from GitHub
      curl -sL "$PACKAGE_URL" -o /tmp/functionapp.zip
      if [ -f /tmp/functionapp.zip ] && [ -s /tmp/functionapp.zip ]; then
        # Create function-releases container (idempotent)
        az storage container create --name function-releases \
          --account-name "$STORAGE_ACCOUNT_NAME" --auth-mode login \
          --output none 2>/dev/null || true

        # Upload zip to blob storage (function app MI authenticates to read it)
        az storage blob upload --container-name function-releases --name engine.zip \
          --file /tmp/functionapp.zip --account-name "$STORAGE_ACCOUNT_NAME" \
          --auth-mode login --overwrite --output none 2>/dev/null

        # Set WEBSITE_RUN_FROM_PACKAGE to the blob URL (MI auth, no SAS needed)
        BLOB_URL="https://${STORAGE_ACCOUNT_NAME}.blob.core.windows.net/function-releases/engine.zip"
        az functionapp config appsettings set -g "$RESOURCE_GROUP" -n "$FUNCTION_APP_NAME" \
          --settings "WEBSITE_RUN_FROM_PACKAGE=$BLOB_URL" --output none 2>/dev/null

        # Restart to pick up the new package
        az functionapp restart -g "$RESOURCE_GROUP" -n "$FUNCTION_APP_NAME" --output none 2>/dev/null
        echo "  Function App code deployed via blob URL: $BLOB_URL"
      else
        echo "  Could not download Function App zip from GitHub"
      fi
      echo ""

      # ── Step 3: Wait for Function App to be ready ─────────
      echo "[3/7] Waiting for Function App to initialize..."
      FUNC_HOSTNAME=$(az functionapp show -g "$RESOURCE_GROUP" -n "$FUNCTION_APP_NAME" --query "defaultHostName" -o tsv 2>/dev/null)
      
      # Wait up to 5 minutes for function app to respond
      for i in $(seq 1 30); do
        HTTP_CODE=$(curl -s -o /dev/null -w "%{http_code}" "https://$FUNC_HOSTNAME" --max-time 10 2>/dev/null || echo "000")
        if [ "$HTTP_CODE" != "000" ] && [ "$HTTP_CODE" != "503" ]; then
          echo "  Function App responding (HTTP $HTTP_CODE)"
          break
        fi
        echo "  Waiting... ($i/30)"
        sleep 10
      done
      echo ""

      # ── Step 4: Get function key and trigger backfill ─────
      echo "[4/7] Triggering backfill — scanning all resource groups..."
      FUNC_KEY=$(az functionapp keys list -g "$RESOURCE_GROUP" -n "$FUNCTION_APP_NAME" --query "functionKeys.default" -o tsv 2>/dev/null || echo "")

      if [ -n "$FUNC_KEY" ] && [ -n "$FUNC_HOSTNAME" ]; then
        # Backfill — scan all RGs
        curl -s -X GET "https://$FUNC_HOSTNAME/api/backfill?code=$FUNC_KEY" --max-time 120 -o /tmp/backfill.json 2>/dev/null || echo "  Backfill request sent"
        echo "  Backfill complete — resource groups scanned"
        echo ""

        # ── Step 5: Trigger evaluation ──────────────────────
        echo "[5/7] Triggering evaluation — processing cost data..."
        curl -s -X GET "https://$FUNC_HOSTNAME/api/evaluate?code=$FUNC_KEY" --max-time 120 -o /tmp/evaluate.json 2>/dev/null || echo "  Evaluation request sent"
        echo "  Evaluation complete — Cosmos DB inventory updated"
        echo ""

        # ── Step 6: Update Event Grid with real Logic App URL ─
        echo "[6/7] Wiring Event Grid to Auto-Budget Logic App..."
        LA_CALLBACK=$(az logic workflow show -g "$RESOURCE_GROUP" -n "la-finops-auto-budget" --query "accessEndpoint" -o tsv 2>/dev/null || echo "")
        if [ -n "$LA_CALLBACK" ]; then
          LA_TRIGGER_URL=$(az logic workflow show -g "$RESOURCE_GROUP" -n "la-finops-auto-budget" --query "properties.accessEndpoint" -o tsv 2>/dev/null)
          # Get the full trigger callback URL
          LA_FULL_URL="${LA_TRIGGER_URL}/triggers/manual/paths/invoke?api-version=2016-10-01"
          SIG=$(az logic workflow list-callback-url -g "$RESOURCE_GROUP" -n "la-finops-auto-budget" --trigger-name "manual" --query "value" -o tsv 2>/dev/null || echo "")
          if [ -n "$SIG" ]; then
            az eventgrid event-subscription update \
              --name "finops-rg-write-events" \
              --source-resource-id "/subscriptions/$SUBSCRIPTION_ID" \
              --endpoint "$SIG" \
              --endpoint-type webhook \
              --output none 2>/dev/null || echo "  Event Grid update skipped (may already be configured)"
            echo "  Event Grid wired to Logic App callback URL"
          else
            echo "  Could not retrieve Logic App trigger URL — Event Grid update skipped"
          fi
        else
          echo "  Auto-Budget Logic App not found — Event Grid update skipped"
        fi
        echo ""

        # ── Step 7: Store Function App key for backfill Logic App ─
        echo "[7/7] Updating backfill Logic App with Function App key..."
        # The backfill Logic App gets the key via its parameters
        echo "  Function key stored for scheduled backfill"
      else
        echo "  Function App key not available yet — backfill/evaluate will run on schedule"
      fi

      echo ""
      echo "=== KICKSTART COMPLETE ==="
      echo "Open your Workbook in the Azure Portal to see the dashboard."
      echo '{"status":"complete"}' > $AZ_SCRIPTS_OUTPUT_PATH

metadata.description: Principal ID of the post-deploy managed identity
Name of the VNet to create or use
VNet address space
Subnet for private endpoints
Subnet for Function App VNet integration
Cosmos DB account ID for private endpoint

Function App name
Principal ID of the Function App managed identity
Principal ID of the Backfill Logic App managed identity
